Face Name Task Behaviour fMRI

Libraries and Functions

# Clear envirnonment
rm(list = ls())

# Get Libraries and Functions
source("~/Dropbox (Personal)/TI_paper/ti-paper/Data/Functions.R")

Import data and add labels

# Import data
setwd("~/Dropbox (Personal)/TI_paper/ti-paper/Data/MRI/FaceNameTask/Behaviour")
data_raw <- read_csv("Behaviour.csv")

# Exclude participant 4 - too much movement in the MRI
data_raw <- subset(data_raw, S_ID!=4)

# Assign appropriate Stim factors
data_raw$StimType <- factor(data_raw$StimType)
levels(data_raw$StimType) <- c("Sham", "TI 1:1", "TI 1:3")

# Assign factors
data_raw$StimType <- factor(data_raw$StimType)
data_raw$block <- factor(data_raw$block)
data_raw$S_ID <- factor(data_raw$S_ID)

Check data for outliers - Accuracy level

g_data <- ddply(data_raw, c("S_ID"), summarise,
         N = sum(!is.na(trial)),
         mean_Acc_prct = (sum(Accuracy, na.rm=TRUE)/N)*100
)


pander(g_data)
S_ID N mean_Acc_prct
1 144 43.75
2 144 32.64
3 144 63.89
5 144 41.67
6 144 57.64
7 144 45.83
8 144 43.75
9 144 57.64
10 144 48.61
11 144 56.25
12 144 33.33
13 144 51.39
14 144 47.22
15 144 50.69
16 144 70.83
18 144 50.69
19 144 61.11
20 144 45.83
21 144 56.25
22 144 40.28 
# All subjects have mean accuracy above 20% (chance level)

ggplot(g_data, aes(x=S_ID, y=mean_Acc_prct)) + 
    geom_bar(position=position_dodge(), stat="identity") 

g_data %>% summarise(mean = mean(mean_Acc_prct),
            sd = sd(mean_Acc_prct), min = min(mean_Acc_prct), max = max(mean_Acc_prct))
##       mean       sd      min      max
## 1 49.96528 9.770336 32.63889 70.83333

Check response distributions

## What is the distribution of responses (correct, foil, distractors, omission)
bdata <-  data_raw
bdata$RespType <- ifelse(bdata$Accuracy==1, "Target", 
       ifelse(bdata$Foil==1, "Foil", 
              ifelse(bdata$Distractor==1, "Distractor", NA)))

# ------- Checking number of cases (percent) -------------

pander(bdata %>% 
    group_by(RespType) %>% 
    summarise(percent = 100 * n() / nrow(bdata)))
RespType percent
Distractor 16.53
Foil 33.33
Target 49.97
NA 0.1736 
# Participants select Correct > Foil > Distractor. They are engaging with the task appropriately. NA = omission

bdata <- bdata[!is.na(bdata$RespType),]  # Getting rid of the few omissions in the dataset
bdata$RespType <- factor(bdata$RespType,levels = c("Target", "Foil", "Distractor"))

Check the distribution of RTs and clean the data if needed

# ---- Plot histogram of RT  --------
ggplot(bdata, aes(x=RT)) + 
 geom_histogram(aes(y=..density..), colour="black", fill="white")+
 geom_density(alpha=.2, fill="#FF6666") 
## `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`.

# Typicall histogram distribution, left skew, right tail


# ----- Check 1 and 99% percentiles and remove those from the data ----------
rts <-  bdata$RT
qts <- quantile(rts, probs = c(.01, .99)) 
qts
##         1%        99% 
##  0.8561875 13.4974937
bdata <- bdata %>% mutate(RT = replace(RT, RT<qts[1], NA))
bdata <- bdata %>% mutate(RT = replace(RT, RT>qts[2], NA))
bdata <- bdata[!is.na(bdata$RT),]

Add additional variables of interest

CorrectRT <-  ifelse(bdata$Accuracy==1, bdata$RT, NA); 
IncorrectRT <- ifelse(bdata$Accuracy==0, bdata$RT, NA); 
CorrectConfidence <- ifelse(bdata$Accuracy==1, bdata$Confidence, NA); 
IncorrectConfidence <- ifelse(bdata$Accuracy==0, bdata$Confidence, NA); 

bdata <- cbind(bdata, CorrectRT, IncorrectRT, CorrectConfidence, IncorrectConfidence)
rm(CorrectRT,IncorrectRT,CorrectConfidence,IncorrectConfidence)

Accuracy 

xtabs( ~ RespType + StimType, bdata)
##             StimType
## RespType     Sham TI 1:1 TI 1:3
##   Target      469    481    476
##   Foil        307    303    326
##   Distractor  156    159    140
ggplot(bdata, aes(x=RespType)) + geom_bar(aes(fill=StimType),position="stack") + 
 ggtitle("Responses per type across stimulation conditions")

# Does the number of responses per response type vary for different stimulation conditions
bdata$RespType <- relevel(bdata$RespType, ref = "Target")
m.acc <- nnet::multinom(RespType ~ StimType, data = bdata)
## # weights:  12 (6 variable)
## initial  value 3094.790817 
## iter  10 value 2830.425815
## iter  10 value 2830.425813
## iter  10 value 2830.425813
## final  value 2830.425813 
## converged
summary(m.acc)
## Call:
## nnet::multinom(formula = RespType ~ StimType, data = bdata)
## 
## Coefficients:
##            (Intercept) StimTypeTI 1:1 StimTypeTI 1:3
## Foil         -0.423760   -0.038369485     0.04524322
## Distractor   -1.100753   -0.006205765    -0.12302250
## 
## Std. Errors:
##            (Intercept) StimTypeTI 1:1 StimTypeTI 1:3
## Foil        0.07341340      0.1037731      0.1027514
## Distractor  0.09242549      0.1300414      0.1333648
## 
## Residual Deviance: 5660.852 
## AIC: 5672.852
exp(coef(m.acc))
##            (Intercept) StimTypeTI 1:1 StimTypeTI 1:3
## Foil         0.6545809      0.9623573      1.0462823
## Distractor   0.3326205      0.9938135      0.8842438
# Check the Z-score for the model (wald Z)
z <- summary(m.acc)$coefficients/summary(m.acc)$standard.errors
z
##            (Intercept) StimTypeTI 1:1 StimTypeTI 1:3
## Foil         -5.772244    -0.36974406      0.4403174
## Distractor  -11.909628    -0.04772145     -0.9224507
p <- (1 - pnorm(abs(z), 0, 1))*2
p
##             (Intercept) StimTypeTI 1:1 StimTypeTI 1:3
## Foil       7.822276e-09      0.7115732      0.6597072
## Distractor 0.000000e+00      0.9619382      0.3562935
Anova(m.acc)
## # weights:  6 (2 variable)
## initial  value 3094.790817 
## final  value 2831.640226 
## converged
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II tests)
## 
## Response: RespType
##          LR Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)
## StimType   2.4288  4     0.6574
emmeans(m.acc, specs = pairwise ~ StimType|RespType, type = "response")
## $emmeans
## RespType = Target:
##  StimType  prob     SE df lower.CL upper.CL
##  Sham     0.503 0.0086  6   0.4822    0.524
##  TI 1:1   0.510 0.0158  6   0.4714    0.549
##  TI 1:3   0.505 0.0296  6   0.4330    0.578
## 
## RespType = Foil:
##  StimType  prob     SE df lower.CL upper.CL
##  Sham     0.329 0.0207  6   0.2788    0.380
##  TI 1:1   0.321 0.0170  6   0.2797    0.363
##  TI 1:3   0.346 0.0298  6   0.2731    0.419
## 
## RespType = Distractor:
##  StimType  prob     SE df lower.CL upper.CL
##  Sham     0.167 0.0176  6   0.1244    0.210
##  TI 1:1   0.169 0.0176  6   0.1255    0.212
##  TI 1:3   0.149 0.0225  6   0.0936    0.204
## 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
## RespType = Target:
##  contrast        estimate     SE df t.ratio p.value
##  Sham - TI 1:1   -0.00685 0.0171  6 -0.401  0.9163 
##  Sham - TI 1:3   -0.00209 0.0286  6 -0.073  0.9971 
##  TI 1:1 - TI 1:3  0.00477 0.0357  6  0.134  0.9902 
## 
## RespType = Foil:
##  contrast        estimate     SE df t.ratio p.value
##  Sham - TI 1:1    0.00808 0.0242  6  0.333  0.9411 
##  Sham - TI 1:3   -0.01667 0.0268  6 -0.623  0.8135 
##  TI 1:1 - TI 1:3 -0.02476 0.0321  6 -0.772  0.7322 
## 
## RespType = Distractor:
##  contrast        estimate     SE df t.ratio p.value
##  Sham - TI 1:1   -0.00123 0.0152  6 -0.081  0.9964 
##  Sham - TI 1:3    0.01876 0.0148  6  1.270  0.4599 
##  TI 1:1 - TI 1:3  0.01999 0.0256  6  0.780  0.7278 
## 
## P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 3 estimates
# No. there is an effect of foil and distractor, but not of stimulation


#----- binomial ----------
m1 <- glmer(Accuracy ~ StimType + (1|S_ID) , data=bdata, family = binomial(link = "logit"), control=glmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5)))

m2<- glmer(Accuracy ~ StimType + (1|S_ID) + (1|block), data=bdata, family = binomial(link = "logit"), control=glmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5)))

m3 <- glmer(Accuracy ~ StimType + (1|S_ID) + (1|block/trial), data=bdata, family = binomial(link = "logit"), control=glmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5)))

anova(m1, m2, m3)
## Data: bdata
## Models:
## m1: Accuracy ~ StimType + (1 | S_ID)
## m2: Accuracy ~ StimType + (1 | S_ID) + (1 | block)
## m3: Accuracy ~ StimType + (1 | S_ID) + (1 | block/trial)
##    npar    AIC    BIC  logLik deviance  Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)   
## m1    4 3860.8 3884.5 -1926.4   3852.8                        
## m2    5 3853.1 3882.8 -1921.5   3843.1 9.6588  1   0.001884 **
## m3    6 3854.4 3890.0 -1921.2   3842.4 0.7392  1   0.389912   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
lmtest:::lrtest(m1, m2, m3)
## Likelihood ratio test
## 
## Model 1: Accuracy ~ StimType + (1 | S_ID)
## Model 2: Accuracy ~ StimType + (1 | S_ID) + (1 | block)
## Model 3: Accuracy ~ StimType + (1 | S_ID) + (1 | block/trial)
##   #Df  LogLik Df  Chisq Pr(>Chisq)   
## 1   4 -1926.4                        
## 2   5 -1921.5  1 9.6588   0.001884 **
## 3   6 -1921.2  1 0.7392   0.389912   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# keeping m2

Anova(m2)
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II Wald chisquare tests)
## 
## Response: Accuracy
##           Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)
## StimType 0.0583  2     0.9713
emmeans(m2, specs = pairwise ~ StimType, type = "response" )
## $emmeans
##  StimType  prob     SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  Sham     0.504 0.0302 Inf     0.445     0.563
##  TI 1:1   0.509 0.0301 Inf     0.451     0.568
##  TI 1:3   0.507 0.0301 Inf     0.448     0.566
## 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## Intervals are back-transformed from the logit scale 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        odds.ratio     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  Sham / TI 1:1        0.978 0.0922 Inf -0.240  0.9687 
##  Sham / TI 1:3        0.987 0.0932 Inf -0.141  0.9891 
##  TI 1:1 / TI 1:3      1.009 0.0953 Inf  0.099  0.9946 
## 
## P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 3 estimates 
## Tests are performed on the log odds ratio scale
summary(m2)
## Generalized linear mixed model fit by maximum likelihood (Laplace
##   Approximation) [glmerMod]
##  Family: binomial  ( logit )
## Formula: Accuracy ~ StimType + (1 | S_ID) + (1 | block)
##    Data: bdata
## Control: glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 2e+05))
## 
##      AIC      BIC   logLik deviance df.resid 
##   3853.1   3882.8  -1921.5   3843.1     2812 
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -1.5963 -0.9596  0.6490  0.9649  1.4718 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  S_ID   (Intercept) 0.1329   0.3645  
##  block  (Intercept) 0.0310   0.1761  
## Number of obs: 2817, groups:  S_ID, 20; block, 9
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)
## (Intercept)     0.01503    0.12063   0.125    0.901
## StimTypeTI 1:1  0.02264    0.09427   0.240    0.810
## StimTypeTI 1:3  0.01329    0.09441   0.141    0.888
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) STTI1:1
## StmTypTI1:1 -0.392        
## StmTypTI1:3 -0.393  0.500
library(DHARMa)
## Warning: package 'DHARMa' was built under R version 3.6.2
## This is DHARMa 0.3.3.0. For overview type '?DHARMa'. For recent changes, type news(package = 'DHARMa') Note: Syntax of plotResiduals has changed in 0.3.0, see ?plotResiduals for details
testDispersion(m2)

## 
##  DHARMa nonparametric dispersion test via sd of residuals fitted vs.
##  simulated
## 
## data:  simulationOutput
## ratioObsSim = 1.0014, p-value = 0.368
## alternative hypothesis: two.sided
simulationOutput <- simulateResiduals(fittedModel = m2, plot = T)

# Checking results on averaged data  for completeness
# --------- lmer - group data ------------

g_bdata <- ddply(bdata, c("S_ID","StimType", "RespType"), summarise,
                  N = sum(!is.na(trial)),
                  meanRespType = N/48, #because there are 48 trials per stimType per participant
                  meanRespTypePerc = meanRespType*100,
                  medianRT = median(RT, na.rm=TRUE),
                  mean_Conf = mean(Confidence, na.rm=TRUE)
)

g_bdata <- g_bdata[!is.na(g_bdata$RespType),]  # Getting rid of the few omissions in the dataset
g_bdata$RespType <- factor(g_bdata$RespType,levels = c("Target", "Foil", "Distractor"))

Resptype.acc <- lmer(meanRespType ~ StimType*RespType + (1|S_ID), data = g_bdata, REML = TRUE)
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
Anova(Resptype.acc, test="F")
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II Wald F tests with Kenward-Roger df)
## 
## Response: meanRespType
##                          F Df Df.res Pr(>F)    
## StimType            0.0270  2    152 0.9734    
## RespType          171.9659  2    152 <2e-16 ***
## StimType:RespType   0.3057  4    152 0.8738    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
summary(Resptype.acc, ddf ="Kenward-Roger")
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Kenward-Roger's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: meanRespType ~ StimType * RespType + (1 | S_ID)
##    Data: g_bdata
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: -276.7
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -2.93953 -0.58581 -0.06277  0.55705  2.62570 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  S_ID     (Intercept) 0.000000 0.00000 
##  Residual             0.009916 0.09958 
## Number of obs: 180, groups:  S_ID, 20
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                     Estimate Std. Error         df t value
## (Intercept)                         0.488542   0.022266 171.000000  21.941
## StimTypeTI 1:1                      0.012500   0.031489 152.000000   0.397
## StimTypeTI 1:3                      0.007292   0.031489 152.000000   0.232
## RespTypeFoil                       -0.168750   0.031489 152.000000  -5.359
## RespTypeDistractor                 -0.326042   0.031489 152.000000 -10.354
## StimTypeTI 1:1:RespTypeFoil        -0.016667   0.044532 152.000000  -0.374
## StimTypeTI 1:3:RespTypeFoil         0.012500   0.044532 152.000000   0.281
## StimTypeTI 1:1:RespTypeDistractor  -0.009375   0.044532 152.000000  -0.211
## StimTypeTI 1:3:RespTypeDistractor  -0.023958   0.044532 152.000000  -0.538
##                                   Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                        < 2e-16 ***
## StimTypeTI 1:1                       0.692    
## StimTypeTI 1:3                       0.817    
## RespTypeFoil                      3.05e-07 ***
## RespTypeDistractor                 < 2e-16 ***
## StimTypeTI 1:1:RespTypeFoil          0.709    
## StimTypeTI 1:3:RespTypeFoil          0.779    
## StimTypeTI 1:1:RespTypeDistractor    0.834    
## StimTypeTI 1:3:RespTypeDistractor    0.591    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) StTTI1:1 StTTI1:3 RspTyF RspTyD STTI1:1:RTF STTI1:3:RTF
## StmTypTI1:1 -0.707                                                        
## StmTypTI1:3 -0.707  0.500                                                 
## RespTypeFol -0.707  0.500    0.500                                        
## RspTypDstrc -0.707  0.500    0.500    0.500                               
## STTI1:1:RTF  0.500 -0.707   -0.354   -0.707 -0.354                        
## STTI1:3:RTF  0.500 -0.354   -0.707   -0.707 -0.354  0.500                 
## STTI1:1:RTD  0.500 -0.707   -0.354   -0.354 -0.707  0.500       0.250     
## STTI1:3:RTD  0.500 -0.354   -0.707   -0.354 -0.707  0.250       0.500     
##             STTI1:1:RTD
## StmTypTI1:1            
## StmTypTI1:3            
## RespTypeFol            
## RspTypDstrc            
## STTI1:1:RTF            
## STTI1:3:RTF            
## STTI1:1:RTD            
## STTI1:3:RTD  0.500     
## optimizer (nloptwrap) convergence code: 0 (OK)
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
### Means to report 
 g_bdata  %>%
  group_by(RespType) %>%
  summarise_at(vars(meanRespType), list(mean = mean, std = sd, SE = se))
## # A tibble: 3 × 4
##   RespType    mean    std     SE
##   <fct>      <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>
## 1 Target     0.495 0.118  0.0152
## 2 Foil       0.325 0.0918 0.0118
## 3 Distractor 0.158 0.0813 0.0105
 g_bdata  %>%
  group_by(StimType, RespType) %>%
  summarise_at(vars(meanRespType), list(mean = mean, std = sd))
## # A tibble: 9 × 4
## # Groups:   StimType [3]
##   StimType RespType    mean    std
##   <fct>    <fct>      <dbl>  <dbl>
## 1 Sham     Target     0.489 0.129 
## 2 Sham     Foil       0.320 0.104 
## 3 Sham     Distractor 0.162 0.0992
## 4 TI 1:1   Target     0.501 0.129 
## 5 TI 1:1   Foil       0.316 0.0982
## 6 TI 1:1   Distractor 0.166 0.0772
## 7 TI 1:3   Target     0.496 0.100 
## 8 TI 1:3   Foil       0.340 0.0731
## 9 TI 1:3   Distractor 0.146 0.0669
### Means per participant

g_bdata2 <- ddply(bdata, c("S_ID","StimType", "Accuracy"), summarise,
                  N = sum(!is.na(trial)),
                  meanRespType = N/48, #because there are 48 trials per stimType per participant
                  meanRespTypePerc = meanRespType*100,
                  medianRT = median(RT, na.rm=TRUE),
                  mean_Conf = mean(Confidence, na.rm=TRUE)
)

CorrectTrials <- subset(g_bdata2, Accuracy==1)

CorrectTrial <- CorrectTrials %>% 
  tibble::as_tibble() %>% 
  group_by(StimType) %>%
  mutate(avg_meanRespTypePerc = mean(meanRespTypePerc)) %>%
  mutate(demeaned_meanRespTypePerc = meanRespTypePerc - avg_meanRespTypePerc)

Reaction Times 

# plotting the data
library(ggridges)
## Warning: package 'ggridges' was built under R version 3.6.2
## 
## Attaching package: 'ggridges'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:tidybayes':
## 
##     scale_point_color_continuous, scale_point_color_discrete,
##     scale_point_colour_continuous, scale_point_colour_discrete,
##     scale_point_fill_continuous, scale_point_fill_discrete,
##     scale_point_size_continuous
ggplot(bdata, aes(x = RT, fill = RespType)) +
                    geom_histogram(bins = 30) +
                    facet_grid(StimType ~ RespType)

# Does reaction time per response type vary for different stimulation conditions?

# Using glm without random effects to find starting values for the optimisation
m0 <- glm(RT ~ StimType*RespType, data=bdata, family = inverse.gaussian(link=identity))

m1 <- glmer(RT ~ StimType*RespType +  (1|S_ID),
            data = bdata,
            family = inverse.gaussian(link=identity),
            start=list(fixef=coef(m0)),
            control=glmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5), nAGQ0initStep=FALSE))

m2 <- glmer(RT ~ StimType*RespType +  (1|S_ID) + (1|block),
            data = bdata,
            family = inverse.gaussian(link=identity),
            start=list(fixef=coef(m0)),
            control=glmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5), nAGQ0initStep=FALSE))


m3 <- glmer(RT ~ StimType*RespType +  (1|S_ID) + (1|block/trial),
            data = bdata,
            family = inverse.gaussian(link=identity),
            start=list(fixef=coef(m0)),
            control=glmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5), nAGQ0initStep=FALSE))


anova(m1, m2, m3)
## Data: bdata
## Models:
## m1: RT ~ StimType * RespType + (1 | S_ID)
## m2: RT ~ StimType * RespType + (1 | S_ID) + (1 | block)
## m3: RT ~ StimType * RespType + (1 | S_ID) + (1 | block/trial)
##    npar   AIC   BIC  logLik deviance   Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## m1   11 11609 11675 -5793.6    11587                          
## m2   12 11508 11579 -5741.9    11484 103.421  1  < 2.2e-16 ***
## m3   13 11469 11546 -5721.3    11443  41.199  1  1.375e-10 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
lmtest:::lrtest(m1, m2, m3)
## Likelihood ratio test
## 
## Model 1: RT ~ StimType * RespType + (1 | S_ID)
## Model 2: RT ~ StimType * RespType + (1 | S_ID) + (1 | block)
## Model 3: RT ~ StimType * RespType + (1 | S_ID) + (1 | block/trial)
##   #Df  LogLik Df   Chisq Pr(>Chisq)    
## 1  11 -5793.6                          
## 2  12 -5741.9  1 103.421  < 2.2e-16 ***
## 3  13 -5721.3  1  41.199  1.375e-10 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# keeping m2
plot(m2)

qqnorm(resid(m2))
qqline(resid(m2))

Anova(m2)
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II Wald chisquare tests)
## 
## Response: RT
##                     Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## StimType           1.0652  2     0.5871    
## RespType          68.2424  2  1.518e-15 ***
## StimType:RespType  6.8863  4     0.1420    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
emmeans(m2, specs = pairwise ~ StimType|RespType, type = "response" )
## $emmeans
## RespType = Target:
##  StimType emmean    SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  Sham       5.24 0.450 Inf      4.36      6.12
##  TI 1:1     5.42 0.450 Inf      4.54      6.31
##  TI 1:3     5.26 0.448 Inf      4.38      6.14
## 
## RespType = Foil:
##  StimType emmean    SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  Sham       5.86 0.456 Inf      4.97      6.75
##  TI 1:1     5.75 0.455 Inf      4.85      6.64
##  TI 1:3     5.89 0.456 Inf      4.99      6.78
## 
## RespType = Distractor:
##  StimType emmean    SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  Sham       5.76 0.462 Inf      4.85      6.67
##  TI 1:1     5.82 0.462 Inf      4.91      6.72
##  TI 1:3     6.14 0.475 Inf      5.21      7.07
## 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
## RespType = Target:
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  Sham - TI 1:1    -0.1832 0.1042 Inf -1.757  0.1842 
##  Sham - TI 1:3    -0.0189 0.0996 Inf -0.190  0.9803 
##  TI 1:1 - TI 1:3   0.1643 0.1040 Inf  1.579  0.2548 
## 
## RespType = Foil:
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  Sham - TI 1:1     0.1156 0.1539 Inf  0.751  0.7329 
##  Sham - TI 1:3    -0.0246 0.1578 Inf -0.156  0.9867 
##  TI 1:1 - TI 1:3  -0.1402 0.1550 Inf -0.904  0.6375 
## 
## RespType = Distractor:
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  Sham - TI 1:1    -0.0544 0.1804 Inf -0.301  0.9511 
##  Sham - TI 1:3    -0.3792 0.2158 Inf -1.757  0.1841 
##  TI 1:1 - TI 1:3  -0.3249 0.2153 Inf -1.509  0.2867 
## 
## P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 3 estimates
# -- Does reaction time for correct vs incorrect vary for different stimulation conditions
m0 <- glm(RT ~ StimType*as.factor(Accuracy), data=bdata, family = inverse.gaussian(link=identity))

m2 <- glmer(RT ~ StimType*as.factor(Accuracy) +  (1|S_ID) + (1|block),
            data = bdata,
            family = inverse.gaussian(link=identity),
            start=list(fixef=coef(m0)),
            control=glmerControl(optimizer="bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun=2e5), nAGQ0initStep=FALSE))

plot(m2)

qqnorm(resid(m2))
qqline(resid(m2))

Anova(m2)
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II Wald chisquare tests)
## 
## Response: RT
##                                Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## StimType                      1.0700  2    0.58567    
## as.factor(Accuracy)          69.2122  1    < 2e-16 ***
## StimType:as.factor(Accuracy)  5.1867  2    0.07477 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
emmeans(m2, specs = pairwise ~ StimType|Accuracy, type = "response" )
## $emmeans
## Accuracy = 0:
##  StimType emmean    SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  Sham       5.82 0.450 Inf      4.94      6.70
##  TI 1:1     5.77 0.450 Inf      4.89      6.66
##  TI 1:3     5.97 0.451 Inf      5.08      6.85
## 
## Accuracy = 1:
##  StimType emmean    SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  Sham       5.24 0.449 Inf      4.36      6.12
##  TI 1:1     5.42 0.449 Inf      4.54      6.30
##  TI 1:3     5.26 0.448 Inf      4.38      6.14
## 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
## Accuracy = 0:
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  Sham - TI 1:1     0.0441 0.1174 Inf  0.376  0.9251 
##  Sham - TI 1:3    -0.1499 0.1272 Inf -1.178  0.4661 
##  TI 1:1 - TI 1:3  -0.1940 0.1253 Inf -1.548  0.2687 
## 
## Accuracy = 1:
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  Sham - TI 1:1    -0.1842 0.1042 Inf -1.767  0.1807 
##  Sham - TI 1:3    -0.0186 0.0997 Inf -0.187  0.9809 
##  TI 1:1 - TI 1:3   0.1656 0.1040 Inf  1.592  0.2492 
## 
## P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 3 estimates
estimated_means <- get_means(m2, "StimType*Accuracy")
pander(estimated_means)
StimType Accuracy Mean SE df CI_lower CI_higher
Sham 0 5.819 0.4504 Inf 4.936 6.702
TI 1:1 0 5.775 0.4503 Inf 4.892 6.657
TI 1:3 0 5.969 0.4515 Inf 5.084 6.854
Sham 1 5.239 0.4491 Inf 4.359 6.12
TI 1:1 1 5.423 0.4493 Inf 4.543 6.304
TI 1:3 1 5.258 0.4479 Inf 4.38 6.136 
pander(bdata %>% 
    group_by(Accuracy) %>% 
    summarise(mean(RT), sd(RT)))
Accuracy mean(RT) sd(RT)
0 5.395 2.492
1 4.427 2.143 
# Checking results on averaged data  for completeness

Resptype.rt <- lmer(medianRT ~ StimType*RespType + (1|S_ID), data = g_bdata, REML = TRUE)
Anova(Resptype.rt, test="F")
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II Wald F tests with Kenward-Roger df)
## 
## Response: medianRT
##                         F Df Df.res    Pr(>F)    
## StimType           0.3069  2    152    0.7362    
## RespType          36.1140  2    152 1.471e-13 ***
## StimType:RespType  0.4716  4    152    0.7565    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
summary(Resptype.rt, ddf ="Kenward-Roger")
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Kenward-Roger's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: medianRT ~ StimType * RespType + (1 | S_ID)
##    Data: g_bdata
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 541.8
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -2.5702 -0.6208 -0.0493  0.5342  3.2841 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  S_ID     (Intercept) 1.9869   1.4096  
##  Residual             0.8424   0.9178  
## Number of obs: 180, groups:  S_ID, 20
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                    Estimate Std. Error        df t value
## (Intercept)                         4.19875    0.37612  34.57883  11.163
## StimTypeTI 1:1                      0.15865    0.29025 152.00000   0.547
## StimTypeTI 1:3                     -0.12371    0.29025 152.00000  -0.426
## RespTypeFoil                        1.14941    0.29025 152.00000   3.960
## RespTypeDistractor                  1.40154    0.29025 152.00000   4.829
## StimTypeTI 1:1:RespTypeFoil        -0.41879    0.41047 152.00000  -1.020
## StimTypeTI 1:3:RespTypeFoil         0.05123    0.41047 152.00000   0.125
## StimTypeTI 1:1:RespTypeDistractor  -0.07210    0.41047 152.00000  -0.176
## StimTypeTI 1:3:RespTypeDistractor  -0.02840    0.41047 152.00000  -0.069
##                                   Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                       5.18e-13 ***
## StimTypeTI 1:1                    0.585465    
## StimTypeTI 1:3                    0.670545    
## RespTypeFoil                      0.000115 ***
## RespTypeDistractor                3.32e-06 ***
## StimTypeTI 1:1:RespTypeFoil       0.309231    
## StimTypeTI 1:3:RespTypeFoil       0.900846    
## StimTypeTI 1:1:RespTypeDistractor 0.860795    
## StimTypeTI 1:3:RespTypeDistractor 0.944921    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) StTTI1:1 StTTI1:3 RspTyF RspTyD STTI1:1:RTF STTI1:3:RTF
## StmTypTI1:1 -0.386                                                        
## StmTypTI1:3 -0.386  0.500                                                 
## RespTypeFol -0.386  0.500    0.500                                        
## RspTypDstrc -0.386  0.500    0.500    0.500                               
## STTI1:1:RTF  0.273 -0.707   -0.354   -0.707 -0.354                        
## STTI1:3:RTF  0.273 -0.354   -0.707   -0.707 -0.354  0.500                 
## STTI1:1:RTD  0.273 -0.707   -0.354   -0.354 -0.707  0.500       0.250     
## STTI1:3:RTD  0.273 -0.354   -0.707   -0.354 -0.707  0.250       0.500     
##             STTI1:1:RTD
## StmTypTI1:1            
## StmTypTI1:3            
## RespTypeFol            
## RspTypDstrc            
## STTI1:1:RTF            
## STTI1:3:RTF            
## STTI1:1:RTD            
## STTI1:3:RTD  0.500
estimated_means <- get_means(Resptype.rt, "StimType*RespType")
pander(estimated_means)
StimType RespType Mean SE df CI_lower CI_higher
Sham Target 4.199 0.3761 34.58 3.435 4.963
TI 1:1 Target 4.357 0.3761 34.58 3.593 5.121
TI 1:3 Target 4.075 0.3761 34.58 3.311 4.839
Sham Foil 5.348 0.3761 34.58 4.584 6.112
TI 1:1 Foil 5.088 0.3761 34.58 4.324 5.852
TI 1:3 Foil 5.276 0.3761 34.58 4.512 6.04
Sham Distractor 5.6 0.3761 34.58 4.836 6.364
TI 1:1 Distractor 5.687 0.3761 34.58 4.923 6.451
TI 1:3 Distractor 5.448 0.3761 34.58 4.684 6.212 
# Save data
save(g_bdata, bdata, file = "BehaviouralData_clean.RData")


### Means to report
 g_bdata  %>%
  group_by(StimType, RespType) %>%
  summarise_at(vars(medianRT), list(mean = mean, std = sd))
## # A tibble: 9 × 4
## # Groups:   StimType [3]
##   StimType RespType    mean   std
##   <fct>    <fct>      <dbl> <dbl>
## 1 Sham     Target      4.20 1.32 
## 2 Sham     Foil        5.35 1.62 
## 3 Sham     Distractor  5.60 1.94 
## 4 TI 1:1   Target      4.36 1.07 
## 5 TI 1:1   Foil        5.09 1.71 
## 6 TI 1:1   Distractor  5.69 2.32 
## 7 TI 1:3   Target      4.08 0.917
## 8 TI 1:3   Foil        5.28 2.00 
## 9 TI 1:3   Distractor  5.45 1.75

Confidence 

xtabs( ~ Confidence + RespType + StimType , bdata)
## , , StimType = Sham
## 
##           RespType
## Confidence Target Foil Distractor
##          0      2    0          0
##          1     91  118         89
##          2    120  102         49
##          3    113   64         15
##          4    143   23          3
## 
## , , StimType = TI 1:1
## 
##           RespType
## Confidence Target Foil Distractor
##          0      1    1          0
##          1    114  126         74
##          2    104  108         55
##          3    112   53         24
##          4    150   15          6
## 
## , , StimType = TI 1:3
## 
##           RespType
## Confidence Target Foil Distractor
##          0      0    1          0
##          1     72  116         60
##          2    132  113         53
##          3    105   75         22
##          4    167   21          5
# Removing confidence = 0 > no response or technical error
sum(bdata$Confidence=="0")
## [1] 5
Confidence_omission_Prct <- (sum(data_raw$Confidence=="0")/length(data_raw$Confidence))*100

bdata <- bdata %>% mutate(Confidence = replace(Confidence, Confidence=="0", NA))
bdata <- bdata[!is.na(bdata$Confidence),] 
bdata$Confidence <- as.factor(bdata$Confidence)

ggplot(data = bdata) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = Confidence, fill = RespType)) + facet_wrap(.~StimType)

# Does the accuracy per response type vary for different stimulation conditions
library(ordinal)
## 
## Attaching package: 'ordinal'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:brms':
## 
##     ranef, VarCorr
## The following objects are masked from 'package:lme4':
## 
##     ranef, VarCorr
## The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     slice
library(ggeffects)
## Warning: package 'ggeffects' was built under R version 3.6.2
m1 = clmm(Confidence ~ StimType*RespType + (1|S_ID), data = bdata, Hess = TRUE)
m2 = clmm(Confidence ~ StimType*RespType + (1|S_ID) + (1|block), data = bdata, Hess = TRUE)
anova(m1,m2)
## Likelihood ratio tests of cumulative link models:
##  
##    formula:                                                    link: threshold:
## m1 Confidence ~ StimType * RespType + (1 | S_ID)               logit flexible  
## m2 Confidence ~ StimType * RespType + (1 | S_ID) + (1 | block) logit flexible  
## 
##    no.par    AIC  logLik LR.stat df Pr(>Chisq)    
## m1     12 7109.6 -3542.8                          
## m2     13 7087.3 -3530.6  24.282  1  8.322e-07 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
library(RVAideMemoire)
## Warning: package 'RVAideMemoire' was built under R version 3.6.2
## *** Package RVAideMemoire v 0.9-79 ***
## 
## Attaching package: 'RVAideMemoire'
## The following object is masked _by_ '.GlobalEnv':
## 
##     se
## The following objects are masked from 'package:ppcor':
## 
##     pcor, pcor.test
## The following object is masked from 'package:lme4':
## 
##     dummy
Anova(m2)
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II tests)
## 
## Response: Confidence
##                   LR Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## StimType             10.43  2   0.005433 ** 
## RespType            420.54  2  < 2.2e-16 ***
## StimType:RespType     5.39  4   0.249476    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
emmeans(m2, specs = pairwise ~ StimType|RespType, type = "response" )
## $emmeans
## RespType = Target:
##  StimType emmean    SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  Sham      0.284 0.166 Inf   -0.0409     0.609
##  TI 1:1    0.213 0.166 Inf   -0.1130     0.538
##  TI 1:3    0.498 0.166 Inf    0.1731     0.822
## 
## RespType = Foil:
##  StimType emmean    SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  Sham     -0.925 0.176 Inf   -1.2703    -0.580
##  TI 1:1   -1.092 0.178 Inf   -1.4410    -0.744
##  TI 1:3   -0.816 0.174 Inf   -1.1567    -0.476
## 
## RespType = Distractor:
##  StimType emmean    SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  Sham     -1.820 0.214 Inf   -2.2392    -1.400
##  TI 1:1   -1.413 0.207 Inf   -1.8190    -1.008
##  TI 1:3   -1.287 0.212 Inf   -1.7020    -0.872
## 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
## RespType = Target:
##  contrast        estimate    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  Sham - TI 1:1     0.0713 0.122 Inf  0.583  0.8294 
##  Sham - TI 1:3    -0.2136 0.122 Inf -1.758  0.1839 
##  TI 1:1 - TI 1:3  -0.2849 0.122 Inf -2.331  0.0516 
## 
## RespType = Foil:
##  contrast        estimate    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  Sham - TI 1:1     0.1672 0.149 Inf  1.125  0.4987 
##  Sham - TI 1:3    -0.1087 0.144 Inf -0.755  0.7307 
##  TI 1:1 - TI 1:3  -0.2760 0.146 Inf -1.891  0.1413 
## 
## RespType = Distractor:
##  contrast        estimate    SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  Sham - TI 1:1    -0.4065 0.216 Inf -1.879  0.1447 
##  Sham - TI 1:3    -0.5325 0.222 Inf -2.396  0.0437 
##  TI 1:1 - TI 1:3  -0.1261 0.215 Inf -0.585  0.8281 
## 
## P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 3 estimates
m3 = clmm(Confidence ~ StimType + (1|S_ID) + (1|block), data = bdata, Hess = TRUE)
Anova(m3)
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II tests)
## 
## Response: Confidence
##          LR Chisq Df Pr(>Chisq)   
## StimType   10.052  2   0.006566 **
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
emmeans(m3, specs = pairwise ~ StimType, type = "response" )
## $emmeans
##  StimType emmean    SE  df asymp.LCL asymp.UCL
##  Sham     -0.442 0.152 Inf    -0.740   -0.1447
##  TI 1:1   -0.476 0.152 Inf    -0.774   -0.1778
##  TI 1:3   -0.229 0.151 Inf    -0.526    0.0676
## 
## Confidence level used: 0.95 
## 
## $contrasts
##  contrast        estimate     SE  df z.ratio p.value
##  Sham - TI 1:1     0.0335 0.0846 Inf  0.397  0.9170 
##  Sham - TI 1:3    -0.2131 0.0841 Inf -2.533  0.0304 
##  TI 1:1 - TI 1:3  -0.2466 0.0847 Inf -2.912  0.0100 
## 
## P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 3 estimates
# Also looking at the average confidence levels for completeness
 
Resptype.conf <- lmer(mean_Conf ~ StimType*RespType + (1|S_ID), data = g_bdata, REML = TRUE)
Anova(Resptype.conf, test="F")
## Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II Wald F tests with Kenward-Roger df)
## 
## Response: mean_Conf
##                         F Df Df.res Pr(>F)    
## StimType           1.2000  2    152 0.3040    
## RespType          91.6232  2    152 <2e-16 ***
## StimType:RespType  0.5821  4    152 0.6761    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
summary(Resptype.conf, ddf ="Kenward-Roger")
## Linear mixed model fit by REML. t-tests use Kenward-Roger's method [
## lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: mean_Conf ~ StimType * RespType + (1 | S_ID)
##    Data: g_bdata
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 240.9
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.7999 -0.5260 -0.0768  0.5301  3.1222 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  S_ID     (Intercept) 0.0896   0.2993  
##  Residual             0.1683   0.4103  
## Number of obs: 180, groups:  S_ID, 20
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                     Estimate Std. Error         df t value
## (Intercept)                         2.626953   0.113558  87.000819  23.133
## StimTypeTI 1:1                      0.000537   0.129734 152.000000   0.004
## StimTypeTI 1:3                      0.129186   0.129734 152.000000   0.996
## RespTypeFoil                       -0.658253   0.129734 152.000000  -5.074
## RespTypeDistractor                 -1.053573   0.129734 152.000000  -8.121
## StimTypeTI 1:1:RespTypeFoil        -0.080717   0.183472 152.000000  -0.440
## StimTypeTI 1:3:RespTypeFoil        -0.081185   0.183472 152.000000  -0.442
## StimTypeTI 1:1:RespTypeDistractor   0.183916   0.183472 152.000000   1.002
## StimTypeTI 1:3:RespTypeDistractor   0.033514   0.183472 152.000000   0.183
##                                   Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                        < 2e-16 ***
## StimTypeTI 1:1                       0.997    
## StimTypeTI 1:3                       0.321    
## RespTypeFoil                      1.12e-06 ***
## RespTypeDistractor                1.47e-13 ***
## StimTypeTI 1:1:RespTypeFoil          0.661    
## StimTypeTI 1:3:RespTypeFoil          0.659    
## StimTypeTI 1:1:RespTypeDistractor    0.318    
## StimTypeTI 1:3:RespTypeDistractor    0.855    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) StTTI1:1 StTTI1:3 RspTyF RspTyD STTI1:1:RTF STTI1:3:RTF
## StmTypTI1:1 -0.571                                                        
## StmTypTI1:3 -0.571  0.500                                                 
## RespTypeFol -0.571  0.500    0.500                                        
## RspTypDstrc -0.571  0.500    0.500    0.500                               
## STTI1:1:RTF  0.404 -0.707   -0.354   -0.707 -0.354                        
## STTI1:3:RTF  0.404 -0.354   -0.707   -0.707 -0.354  0.500                 
## STTI1:1:RTD  0.404 -0.707   -0.354   -0.354 -0.707  0.500       0.250     
## STTI1:3:RTD  0.404 -0.354   -0.707   -0.354 -0.707  0.250       0.500     
##             STTI1:1:RTD
## StmTypTI1:1            
## StmTypTI1:3            
## RespTypeFol            
## RspTypDstrc            
## STTI1:1:RTF            
## STTI1:3:RTF            
## STTI1:1:RTD            
## STTI1:3:RTD  0.500
estimated_means <- get_means(Resptype.conf, "StimType*RespType")
pander(estimated_means)
StimType RespType Mean SE df CI_lower CI_higher
Sham Target 2.627 0.1136 87 2.401 2.853
TI 1:1 Target 2.627 0.1136 87 2.402 2.853
TI 1:3 Target 2.756 0.1136 87 2.53 2.982
Sham Foil 1.969 0.1136 87 1.743 2.194
TI 1:1 Foil 1.889 0.1136 87 1.663 2.114
TI 1:3 Foil 2.017 0.1136 87 1.791 2.242
Sham Distractor 1.573 0.1136 87 1.348 1.799
TI 1:1 Distractor 1.758 0.1136 87 1.532 1.984
TI 1:3 Distractor 1.736 0.1136 87 1.51 1.962 
estimated_means <- get_means(Resptype.conf, "StimType")
## NOTE: Results may be misleading due to involvement in interactions
pander(estimated_means)
StimType Mean SE df CI_lower CI_higher
Sham 2.056 0.08535 33.88 1.883 2.23
TI 1:1 2.091 0.08535 33.88 1.918 2.265
TI 1:3 2.17 0.08535 33.88 1.996 2.343 
### Means to report
 g_bdata  %>%
  group_by(StimType, RespType) %>%
  summarise_at(vars(mean_Conf), list(mean = mean, std = sd))
## # A tibble: 9 × 4
## # Groups:   StimType [3]
##   StimType RespType    mean   std
##   <fct>    <fct>      <dbl> <dbl>
## 1 Sham     Target      2.63 0.335
## 2 Sham     Foil        1.97 0.555
## 3 Sham     Distractor  1.57 0.586
## 4 TI 1:1   Target      2.63 0.474
## 5 TI 1:1   Foil        1.89 0.492
## 6 TI 1:1   Distractor  1.76 0.471
## 7 TI 1:3   Target      2.76 0.357
## 8 TI 1:3   Foil        2.02 0.578
## 9 TI 1:3   Distractor  1.74 0.638

Plots - Figure 1C

cbPalette <- c("#E69F00", "#D55E00","#CC79A7", "#999999")

levels(bdata$StimType)[levels(bdata$StimType)=="Sham"] <- "Sham"
levels(bdata$StimType)[levels(bdata$StimType)=="TI 1:1"] <- "TI 1:1"
levels(bdata$StimType)[levels(bdata$StimType)=="TI 1:3"] <- "TI 1:3"

library(scales)
# Plot response type 


bdata$Confidence <- as.numeric(bdata$Confidence)

g_bdata <- ddply(bdata, c("S_ID","StimType", "RespType"), summarise,
                  N = sum(!is.na(trial)),
                  meanRespType = N/48, #because there are 48 trials per stimType per participant
                  meanRespTypePerc = meanRespType*100,
                  medianRT = median(RT, na.rm=TRUE),
                  mean_Conf = mean(Confidence, na.rm=TRUE)
)


p.RespType <- ggplot(g_bdata, aes(x = RespType, y = meanRespTypePerc, fill = StimType))  + 
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  stat_summary(geom = "errorbar", fun.data = mean_se, position = position_dodge(0.9), size = 0.6, width = 0.3, colour="black") + geom_dotplot(binaxis='y', stackdir='center', position=position_dodge(0.9), stackratio=1, dotsize=0.15) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Mean Response (%)",
                           breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),
                           limits=c(0, 100)) +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(color="black", size=14, face="bold", hjust = 0.5),
          axis.text.x = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 0, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 0, face = "plain"),  
        axis.title.x = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 0, face = "bold"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 90, face = "bold"),
            legend.text = element_blank(),
        legend.background = element_blank(),
        legend.key=element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        legend.title=element_blank()) +
   scale_x_discrete(breaks=c("Target","Foil", "Distractor"),
        labels=c("Target","Foil", "Distractor")) +
         theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), axis.line = element_line(colour = "black")) + 
    theme(strip.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
          strip.text.y = element_text(size = 20),
          legend.text = element_text(size=14),
          legend.key.size = unit(0.5, 'cm')) + ggtitle("") + xlab("")  + scale_fill_manual(values = c("#747674", "#56B4E9", "#D16103"), name = "", labels = c("Sham", "TI 1:1", "TI 1:3"))

ggsave(p.RespType ,file="Figure_2C.tiff", width = 12, height = 9, dpi = 300, units = "cm", device = "tiff")  

# alternative
p.RespType.alt <- ggplot(bdata, aes(x = StimType, 
                  fill = factor(RespType, levels = c("Target", "Foil", "Distractor")))) + 
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, .2), 
                     label = percent) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cbPalette) +
  labs(y = "Percent", fill = "Response",
       x = "Stimulation",
       title = "") +
  theme_minimal() + theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 12), axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
                          legend.text = element_text(size = 12), 
                          legend.title = element_blank(),
                          axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                          axis.text.x.top = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", color = "black"),
                          panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), panel.background = element_blank()) + 
  scale_x_discrete(position = "top") + ylab("Response")




### Plot RT

p.RespType.RT <- ggplot(g_bdata, aes(x = RespType, y = medianRT, fill = StimType))  + 
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  stat_summary(geom = "errorbar", fun.data = mean_se, position = position_dodge(0.9), size = 0.6, width = 0.3, colour="black") + geom_dotplot(binaxis='y', stackdir='center', position=position_dodge(0.9), stackratio=1, dotsize=0.15) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Median RT (s)",
                           breaks = seq(0, 12, 2),
                           limits=c(0, 12)) +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(color="black", size=14, face="bold", hjust = 0.5),
          axis.text.x = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 0, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 0, face = "plain"),  
        axis.title.x = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 0, face = "bold"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 90, face = "bold"),
            legend.text = element_blank(),
        legend.background = element_blank(),
        legend.key=element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        legend.title=element_blank()) +
   scale_x_discrete(breaks=c("Target","Foil", "Distractor"),
        labels=c("Target","Foil", "Distractor")) +
         theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), axis.line = element_line(colour = "black")) + 
    theme(strip.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
          strip.text.y = element_text(size = 20),
          legend.text = element_text(size=14),
          legend.key.size = unit(0.5, 'cm')) + ggtitle("") + xlab("")  + scale_fill_manual(values = c("#747674", "#56B4E9", "#D16103"), name = "", labels = c("Sham", "TI 1:1", "TI 1:3"))


# Plot RT distribution
library(ggridges)

bdata$RespType <- factor(bdata$RespType, levels = c("Distractor", "Foil", "Target"), ordered = TRUE)

p.RT <- ggplot(bdata, aes(x = RT, y = StimType, fill = RespType)) +
  geom_density_ridges(scale = 0.8, alpha = 0.6) +
  theme_ridges(grid = FALSE, center_axis_labels = TRUE) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#E69F00", "#D55E00", "#CC79A7"),
                       name="Response",
                       breaks=c("Target", "Foil", "Distractor"),
                       labels=c("Target", "Foil", "Distractor")) +
 theme(axis.title.y = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
       legend.title = element_blank()) + 
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Reaction Time (s)") + scale_y_discrete(expand = c(0, 0.2))



# Plot confidence

level_order <- factor(g_bdata$RespType, levels = c("Target", "Foil", "Distractor"))

p.RespType.Conf <- ggplot(g_bdata, aes(x =level_order, y = mean_Conf, fill = StimType))  + 
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  stat_summary(geom = "errorbar", fun.data = mean_se, position = position_dodge(0.9), size = 0.6, width = 0.3, colour="black") + geom_dotplot(binaxis='y', stackdir='center', position=position_dodge(0.9), stackratio=1, dotsize=0.15) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Mean Confidence",
                           breaks = seq(0, 4, 1),
                           limits=c(0, 4)) +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(color="black", size=14, face="bold", hjust = 0.5),
          axis.text.x = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 0, face = "bold"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 0, face = "plain"),  
        axis.title.x = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 0, face = "bold"),
        axis.title.y = element_text(color = "grey20", size = 14, angle = 90, face = "bold"),
            legend.text = element_blank(),
        legend.background = element_blank(),
        legend.key=element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        legend.title=element_blank()) +
   scale_x_discrete(breaks=c("Target","Foil", "Distractor"),
        labels=c("Target","Foil", "Distractor")) +
         theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), axis.line = element_line(colour = "black")) + 
    theme(strip.text.x = element_text(size = 14),
          strip.text.y = element_text(size = 20),
          legend.text = element_text(size=14),
          legend.key.size = unit(0.5, 'cm')) + ggtitle("") + xlab("")  + scale_fill_manual(values = c("#747674", "#56B4E9", "#D16103"), name = "", labels = c("Sham", "TI 1:1", "TI 1:3"))


# Alternative
p.Confidence <- ggplot(bdata, aes(x = factor(RespType, 
                         levels = c("Target", "Foil", "Distractor")),
                  fill = factor(Confidence, levels = c("4", "3", "2", "1")))) + 
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, .2), 
                     label = percent) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = cbPalette)  +
  labs(y = "Percent", fill = "Confidence",
       x = "",
       title = "") +
  theme_minimal() + theme(text = element_text(size = 14), 
                          axis.text = element_text(size = 12), 
                          axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
                          legend.text = element_text(size = 12), 
                          legend.title = element_blank(),
                          axis.title.x = element_blank(),
                          axis.text.x.top = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
                          axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
                          panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), 
                          panel.background = element_blank()) + ylab("Confidence") + facet_grid(~StimType) + theme(strip.text.x = element_text(size = 14, face="bold"))



p.RespType.alt

p.RT

p.Confidence

p.RespType

p.RespType.RT

p.RespType.Conf

# Combine Plots
p.Behav <- grid.arrange(p.RespType, p.RespType.RT, p.RT, p.RespType.Conf, nrow = 1, ncol=4, widths = c(2,2,1.8,2))

ggsave(p.Behav, filename = "Figure_S3.tiff", width = 40, height = 10, dpi = 300, units = "cm", device = "tiff")
